      "source": [
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split\n",
        "from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression\n",
        "from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, r2_score"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "data=pd.read_csv(\"/content/car data.csv\")\n",
        "print(data.head())"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "E04V58FUgZSR",
        "outputId": "a694d1bf-031d-4ed4-c1c8-88ad25a3b0a3"
      },
      "execution_count": 2,

    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "print(data.columns)"
      ],

    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "print(data.info())\n",
        "print(data.isnull().sum())"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "UoZv1dDXhG-F",
        "outputId": "34a29879-f1a8-4829-89cf-9d6bc8e33e81"

            "Fuel_Type        0\n",
            "Selling_type     0\n",
            "Transmission     0\n",
            "Owner            0\n",
            "dtype: int64\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "data[\"Fuel_Type\"]=data[\"Fuel_Type\"].map({\n",
        "    \"Petrol\":0,\n",
        "    \"Diesel\":1,\n",
        "    \"CNG\":2\n",
        "}).fillna(3)\n",
        "data[\"Selling_type\"]=data[\"Selling_type\"].map({\n",
        "    \"Dealer\":0,\n",
        "    \"Individual\":1\n",
        "}).fillna(2)\n",
        "data[\"Transmission\"]=data[\"Transmission\"].map({\n",
        "    \"Manual\":0,\n",
        "    \"Automatic\":1\n",
        "}).fillna(2)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "wTjGYl0zhfhI"
      },
      "execution_count": 5,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "x=data.drop(\"Car_Name\",axis=1)\n",
        "y=data[\"Selling_Price\"]\n",
        "x=x.drop(\"Selling_Price\",axis=1)\n",
        "x_train,x_test,y_train,y_test=train_test_split(x,y,test_size=0.2,random_state=42)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "oaTNEbzNiHVD"
      },
      "execution_count": 6,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "model=LinearRegression()\n",
        "model.fit(x_train,y_train)\n",
        "y_pred = model.predict(x_test)\n",
        "print(y_pred)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "OJqdp44difwX",
        "outputId": "19b0b2cd-89b8-488c-9a95-b268fdde8d62"
      },
      "execution_count": 7,

          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "mae=mean_absolute_error(y_test,y_pred)\n",
        "r2=r2_score(y_test,y_pred)\n",
        "print(\"Mean Absolute Error:\",mae)\n",
        "print(\"R2 Score:\",r2)"
      ],
      "metadata": {

          ]

    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "result=pd.DataFrame({\n",
        "    \"Actual Price\":y_test,\n",
        "    \"Predicted Price\":y_pred\n",
        "})\n",

    {
      "cell_type": "code",

        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "**Conclusion**\n",
        "# In this project, I built a car price prediction model using machine learning. I used a dataset containing different features like fuel type, transmission, selling type, and car details. First, I cleaned and converted the categorical data into numerical values so that the model could understand it.Then I split the data into training and testing sets and trained a Linear Regression model to predict the selling price of cars. After training, I tested the model and compared the actual prices with the predicted prices.Finally, I evaluated the model using Mean Absolute Error and R2 score, and also visualized the results using a scatter plot. The model performed reasonably well and was able to predict car prices close to the actual values.Overall, this project helped me understand how machine learning can be used for real-world price prediction problems."
      ],

        "id": "xhk6QBN3lOz6"
